scope: Put / Update existing POD (POD name only) – Technical User Story from Confluence. Updates only the name of an existing Point of Delivery on the current version; does not create a new version.
entry_points: PUT /api/pod/{id} – path id = POD DB id; body: versionId (Integer), name (String, 1–1024 chars, non-blank). Response: 200 with { id, podDetailId, versionId }. Validations: 400 for validation failures; 404 when POD or POD version not found.
point-of-delivery-controller (OpenAPI: edit_39) – existing PUT /pod/{id} in backend-architecture; may be extended or a new name-only variant (e.g. PodNameUpdateRequest) per story.

upstream.name: pod.pod
upstream.usage: POD entity; path {id} refers to pod.id

upstream.name: pod.pod_details
upstream.usage: Only pod_details.name is updated; versionId in request identifies the current version

upstream.name: PointOfDeliveryController
upstream.usage: Exposes PUT /pod/{id}; reuse or extend for name-only update

upstream.name: PointOfDeliveryService
upstream.usage: Business logic for POD update; must update only name on existing version

upstream.name: PointOfDeliveryDetailsRepository
upstream.usage: Persistence for pod_details; update of name field

upstream.name: PodResponse
upstream.usage: Response DTO: id, podDetailId, versionId (from OpenAPI)

upstream.name: PodUpdateRequest or PodNameUpdateRequest
upstream.usage: Request DTO: versionId, name (1–1024, non-blank); current PodUpdateRequest has many required fields—name-only may need new DTO or overload

upstream.name: Auth / permissions
upstream.usage: Sales portal user must have permission to update POD (e.g. Sales agent)

downstream.name: Sales portal
downstream.usage: Triggers API when user confirms (record) update on existing POD data; re-contracting flow; only POD name is updated

downstream.name: GET /pod/{id}
downstream.usage: Returns POD details; callers will see updated name after PUT

downstream.name: GET /pod/list
downstream.usage: Search/listing; POD name may appear in results

downstream.name: contract-pod-controller, product-contract, service-contract, express-contract
downstream.usage: Contract–POD associations; contract screens and GET /pod/contract/{identifier} may display or use POD name

downstream.name: Phoenix UI (contract preview, POD tabs)
downstream.usage: Displays POD data; name change reflected after update

downstream.name: Billing / invoice / termination flows
downstream.usage: Reference POD; reports or exports that use pod_details.name may change after update

integration_points: Sales portal → PUT /api/pod/{id} (trigger on confirm update/actualization)
Contract–POD association (product/service/express contract screens and APIs that show POD name)

what_could_break.item: Existing PUT /pod/{id} callers sending full PodUpdateRequest
what_could_break.reason: If backend switches to name-only (PodNameUpdateRequest) or adds validation that rejects missing optional fields, existing clients could get 400
Sales portal or other callers sending empty name or length > 1024 will get 400; must align portal validation

what_could_break.item: 404 when POD or version not found
what_could_break.reason: Invalid id or versionId returns 404; Sales portal must handle and display appropriately

what_could_break.item: Contract and termination flows that display POD name
what_could_break.reason: UI or reports that cache or display pod name should reflect update; no new version created so only current version name changes

what_could_break.item: Billing run / invoice / SLP logic that use pod_details
what_could_break.reason: Only name is updated; if any logic incorrectly depended on name for billing/invoice key, it could be affected (unlikely for name-only)
Listings that show POD name will show new name after update

what_could_break.location: EnergoTS fixtures, tests that create or update POD
what_could_break.reason: New or changed request body (versionId + name only) may require test updates

technical_details: No Jira key provided; no merge lookup performed (Rule 35a applies only when Jira/bug/task key is given). Confluence source: Phoenix space, page id 740229121, title 'Put Update existing POD'. Business rule: only data customer can update is POD name; update happens on existing POD version (from request); system does not create new version. Current OpenAPI (backend-architecture.json): point-of-delivery-controller has PUT /pod/{id} (edit_39), models PodUpdateRequest (required: consumptionPurpose, estimatedMonthlyAvgConsumption, name, type, voltageLevel; name minLength 1, maxLength 1024; versionId in validation_rules) and PodResponse (id, podDetailId, versionId). Implementation may introduce PodNameUpdateRequest (versionId, name only) or restrict existing PUT to name-only for Sales portal path. Backend: pod.pod, pod.pod_details; only pod_details.name updated. Reuse: PointOfDeliveryController, PointOfDeliveryService, PointOfDeliveryDetailsRepository.
